articles.title: Build a Feedback Channel That Does Not Become Another Abandoned Inbox
articles.excerpt: A feedback channel is easy to launch: add an email address, form, or chat link. The difficult part is operating it six months later. Messages become abandoned when their destination is disconnected fr…
articles.body: A feedback channel is easy to launch: add an email address, form, or chat link. The difficult part is operating it six months later. Messages become abandoned when their destination is disconnected from the maintainers’ normal work. The solution is not necessarily a better inbox. It is a small routing system with explicit ownership, visible states, and a predictable triage schedule. This tutorial builds that system for an open-source repository using: a contact page that routes each request to the right channel; GitHub Issue Forms for actionable bug reports; a support policy with response expectations; labels that represent workflow state; a scheduled GitHub Action that detects overdue triage.
articles.author: LunarDrift

articles.title: Designing Idempotent Stripe Webhook Delivery for Generated Files
articles.excerpt: A successful payment is not the same thing as a successful order. For a SaaS product that generates a personalized file after checkout, fulfillment usually crosses several systems: the browser, your d…
articles.body: A successful payment is not the same thing as a successful order. For a SaaS product that generates a personalized file after checkout, fulfillment usually crosses several systems: the browser, your database, Stripe, a PDF renderer, object storage, an email provider, and a download endpoint. Any one of them can fail independently. We recently hardened this flow for a document-generation product. The most useful lesson was to stop thinking of the webhook as “the function that sends the PDF” and start treating it as an event that advances a persisted state machine. The fragile version A tempting first implementation looks like this: if ( event . type === " checkout.session.completed " ) { const pdf = await generatePdf ( session . metadata ); await sendEmail ( session .
articles.author: Stvens Matin

articles.title: A Practical Three-State Validation Model for User-Generated PDFs
articles.excerpt: Most form validation libraries answer one question: is this field valid? Document-generation products need to answer a harder question: is this file safe to export, and if it is technically exportable…
articles.body: Most form validation libraries answer one question: is this field valid? Document-generation products need to answer a harder question: is this file safe to export, and if it is technically exportable, does a human still need to review something? We ran into this problem while building a print-oriented web app. The inputs included structured facts, free-form text, photos, template choices, and page-format decisions. A green check beside every form field did not guarantee a usable PDF. The model that worked for us has three states: type CheckerStatus = " pass " | " needs_review " | " cannot_export " ; This article explains why three states are better than two and how to connect them to a real rendering pipeline.
articles.author: Stvens Matin

articles.title: We turned agent conversations into git commits (and it's actually useful)
articles.excerpt: Ever wished your agent conversations were as trackable as your code? Gitlord makes it real. What it does: Every agent turn becomes a git commit Branch out subagents without breaking your main flow Rew…
articles.body: Ever wished your agent conversations were as trackable as your code? Gitlord makes it real. What it does: Every agent turn becomes a git commit Branch out subagents without breaking your main flow Rewind to any point in your conversation history Connect tools via MCP (filesystem, search, browser, etc.) One interface, any AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models) Full CLI for managing sessions and branches Why it matters: Your agent history is navigable, forkable, and diffable, just like code Context management handles token budgets automatically Spawn child agents on isolated branches with their own history No lock-in: all components are modular and swappable Built-in integrations: MCP tools: Connect any MCP server (git, filesystem, browser, search).
articles.author: Yashneil

articles.title: How POS, PMS, Middleware, and ERP Integration Improve Hospitality Data Flow
articles.excerpt: Hotels and restaurants often rely on different systems for daily operations. A Point-of-Sale system (POS) manages restaurant orders and payments. A Property Management System (PMS) handles hotel reser…
articles.body: Hotels and restaurants often rely on different systems for daily operations. A Point-of-Sale system (POS) manages restaurant orders and payments. A Property Management System (PMS) handles hotel reservations, guest records, and front-office activities. An Enterprise Resource Planning system (ERP) supports accounting, inventory, purchasing, and reporting. The challenge is moving information between these platforms. When systems are disconnected, employees may need to export files, re-enter transactions, and compare reports manually. This can slow down workflows and create inconsistent records. Middleware helps solve this problem by acting as a connection layer.
articles.author: Jinisys Software Inc.

articles.title: Topobuilder brings a cPanel website into an AWS multi-region containerized application with an HAProxy load balancer
articles.excerpt: In 2024, I built a piece of software called topobuilder in Go. I've referenced it in interviews often enough that it deserves a public page to point at — so here's what it is and how I built it, told…
articles.body: In 2024, I built a piece of software called topobuilder in Go. I've referenced it in interviews often enough that it deserves a public page to point at — so here's what it is and how I built it, told through fragments of a closed-source codebase. What Is Topobuilder? topobuilder is a go binary that runs off a single YAML configuration file that defines an existing PHP website hosted on a cPanel server. The defaults assume that any database connectivity is going to be performed over RDS or another remote connection . What you're doing is going from a single region into a multi-region load balanced containerized application. It takes about 12-36 minutes to deploy a 3-6-9 region cluster with an HAProxy load balancer.

articles.excerpt: Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made…
articles.body: Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made one thing clear: Tesla still has no concrete plan. Instead of committing to a fix, Musk floated a grab-bag of options and timelines — none of them firm — for the millions of vehicles Tesla sold with the promise that they had “all the hardware necessary” for self-driving.

articles.excerpt: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle, Galaxy Explore…
articles.body: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant . He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle , Galaxy Explorer , and Pirates of Barracuda Bay . Now, he's helped the company create a $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto's favor - or so he's told. It doesn't play quite like Donkey Kong , of course. Jumpman, the character that would be renamed Mario, can never reach the top under his own power. But like Lego's $230 pinball machine , it does contain a basic fully functional game. There's a working joyst … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.body: It’s finally official: LEGO has announced a set based on the classic Donkey Kong arcade cabinet. After a leak or two, LEGO Donkey Kong Arcade is officially scheduled for release on August 1, with a price tag of $199.99. You can see it at the LEGO Store here . LEGO Donkey Kong Arcade Release Date: August 1, 2026 MSRP: $199.99 Pieces: 1,367 Ages: 18+ Set #: 72051 LEGO Donkey Kong Arcade is comprised of 1,367 pieces and contains many moving parts. Because of the retro subject matter and the complexity of the build, LEGO Donkey Kong Arcade is aimed at the 18+ age bracket, making it one of the many LEGO sets for adults . Once assembled, it becomes an interactive game that looks remarkably like the original arcade cabinet.

articles.title: The Odyssey’s most important visual was inspired by a 58-year-old twist from a classic sci-fi movie
articles.excerpt: Christopher Nolan has been thinking about how to film the Trojan Horse for decades
articles.body: The Trojan horse is one of those indelible images. It’s a tale that most of us are acquainted with, whether we studied it in school, saw it in a movie, read about it in books, or heard it mentioned in a song . In Greek mythology, the Trojan horse was a trick devised by Odysseus in the Trojan War, resulting in the end of a struggle that had dragged on for a decade. The giant wooden horse was presented as a peace offering to the Trojans and was dragged inside the city's impregnable walls, only for Greek warriors to crawl out of it and destroy the city in one night.

articles.title: Unlikely Pokémon Winds & Waves monster hides 20 years of history
articles.excerpt: A small detail in Pokémon Pokopia might've been planned for Diamond & Pearl. And we may not see the full version until the Switch 2 Pokémon games
articles.body: Pokémon archivists just released footage of a never-before-seen documentary about the making of 2007's Pokémon Diamond and Pokémon Pearl , and it's proven to be a treasure trove of trivia for hardcore fans. There's an inside look at how Game Freak develops starters, early Pokémon designs for some of the most iconic characters, and surprisingly, tidbits that shed light on modern Pokémon games. Developers at Game Freak came up with ideas 20 years ago that did not see the light of day until the release of Pokémon Pokopia . And the idea might reappear when Pokémon Winds and Waves is released next year.

articles.body: Dell is offering a steep discount on its most powerful desktop computer. The Alienware Area-51 AMD Ryzen X3D edition gaming PC equipped with a GeForce RTX 5090 GPU and 64GB of DDR5-6400MHz RAM has dropped to $5,399.99 shipped after a $1,500 instant discount. No, it's still not even remotely affordable for most people, but if you've got deep pockets, this is a substantial discount on a beastly Alienware system. If you look at the prices of the individual components in today's market, it's (sadly) not as ridiculous as your initial sticker shock might lead you to believe. Alienware Area-51 Ryzen X3D RTX 5090 Gaming PC w/ 64GB RAM The Alienware Area-51 is Dell's flagship gaming PC.

articles.title: Your Google Meet files are finally getting the organization they need
articles.excerpt: Google Meet is giving notes, recordings, and transcripts a proper home with automatically organized folders in Google Drive.
articles.body: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR Google Meet will automatically organize notes, transcripts, and recordings in a new Google Meet folder in Drive. Each meeting gets its own subfolder, while recurring meetings will keep all instances together. Attendees with access will also see shortcuts, with the rollout starting today. Yesterday, we saw that Google Meet is making it easier to find the files associated with your calls on its redesigned web homepage . Google is now tackling the same problem on the Drive side, with meeting notes, transcripts, and recordings set to be organized automatically rather than left loose in the host’s storage.

Mares has partnered with Samsung for a Diving app, and we’ve got an early look at how its subscription could work. Samsung just got done showing off all its new summer hardware at Unpacked in London, including the wide new Galaxy Z Fold 8 , Fold 8 Ultra , and Flip 8 . More than that, though, we also got to meet Samsung’s latest wearables, with the launch of the Galaxy Watch 9 and Watch Ultra 2 . The Ultra 2 in particular is packed with some serious features, and beyond the big hardware upgrades, it’s also got a nice set of software exclusives — including an upcoming Diving app from Mares.

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.excerpt: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.
articles.body: The world's most powerful solid-fuel rocket just aced its third-ever mission. Gravity-1, a distinctively squat and stubby rocket , launched Tuesday night (July 21) from the deck of a ship stationed off the coast of Shanghai. Liftoff occurred at 10:54 p.m. EDT (0254 GMT and 10:54 a.m. Beijing time on July 22), according to the state-run Chinese outlet Xinhua, which further reported that all nine satellites reached "their designated orbits." Details about those satellites were not provided. Orienspace's Gravity-1 rocket launches nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea on July 21, 2026. (Image credit: CCTV) Gravity-1 is operated by Orienspace, a company based in the eastern Chinese province of Shandong.

articles.title: 5 disturbing things we saw after digging into the recent 'Dune: Part 3' trailer
articles.excerpt: Let's explore this latest look at Denis Villeneuve's epic close-out to his 'Dune' magnum opus
articles.body: The latest " Dune: Part 3 " trailer arrived back on July 8, and it was a sumptuous banquet of startling set pieces, intergalactic political unrest, close-quarter combat, and a whole lot of sand and ash. A fine follow-up to the initial March teaser , to be sure. After letting it marinate in our minds for a while, let’s break down five items of particular interest in this newest preview of the Denis Villeneuve-directed conclusion to his trilogy of far-future sci-fi epics adapted from author Frank Herbert’s novels, "Dune" and "Dune: Messiah." Set 17 years after Paul Atreides (Timothée Chalamet) has ascended to the Emperor’s throne, "Dune: Part 3" explores the corrupting side of political power as his holy war against the Imperium’s Great Houses advances towards a destructive destiny.

articles.title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
articles.excerpt: Astronomers discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet that could help scientists identify more hidden comets and improve planetary defense.
articles.body: A near-Earth object that astronomers believed was an asteroid for nearly three decades has been unmasked as a faint, active comet, revealing a case of cosmic mistaken identity that could help improve planetary defense. The object, known since its discovery in 1998 as 1998 SH2, appeared to be an ordinary asteroid . It follows a 4.5-year orbit around the sun and showed no obvious signs of cometary activity, such as the glowing coma or tail produced when sunlight vaporizes surface ice. However, during a close pass about 2 million miles (3 million kilometers) from Earth in August 2025, researchers using NASA's Deep Space Network planetary radar system noticed something unexpected: The object wasn't where orbital predictions said it should be, according to a statement from the space agency.
articles.author: Samantha Mathewson

articles.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
articles.excerpt: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s…
articles.body: The Flight Dynamics Research Facility, located at NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, is the agency’s first major wind tunnel built in more than 40 years. NASA/Mark Knopp Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s first new wind tunnel in more than 40 years. The event will include a brief media availability with: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman Dr. Trina Dyal, center director, NASA Langley Administrator Edward C. Forst, U.S. General Services Administration This event is in person only and open to members of the media who are United States citizens or lawful permanent residents.
articles.author: Jennifer M. Dooren

articles.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
articles.excerpt: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent i…
articles.body: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms. GitHub said the
articles.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

articles.title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
articles.excerpt: The FCC voted July 22 to push satellite operators out of more C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year, while approving a licensing overhaul aimed at accelerating the commercial space economy. The p…
articles.body: The FCC voted July 22 to push satellite operators out of more C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year, while approving a licensing overhaul aimed at accelerating the commercial space economy. The post FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G appeared first on SpaceNews .

articles.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
articles.excerpt: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete c…
articles.body: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default installations of Ubuntu Desktop 24.04, 25.10, and 26.04. The disclosure comes as
articles.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

articles.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)
articles.excerpt: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not i…
articles.body: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series D, and Murphy has had a front-row seat as the company went from a pre-revenue, […]
